decks.name: Reading Time on a Clock
decks.description: Learn how to read hours and minutes on a clock face

cards.front: What do the two hands on a clock show?
cards.back:
The short (small) hand shows the hour.
The long (big) hand shows the minutes.

When the short hand points to 3 and the long hand points to 12, the time is 3 o'clock.
cards.hint: The short hand moves slowly (hours), the long hand moves fast (minutes)

cards.front: How many minutes does the long hand take to go from one number to the next?
cards.back:
The long (minute) hand takes 5 minutes to move from one number to the next on the clock.

12 to 1 = 5 minutes
12 to 2 = 10 minutes
12 to 3 = 15 minutes
12 to 6 = 30 minutes
12 all the way around to 12 = 60 minutes (1 hour)
cards.hint: Count by 5s as you go around the clock: 5, 10, 15, 20...

cards.front: What time does the clock show when the long hand is on 6?
cards.back:
When the long hand points to 6, it means 30 minutes past the hour. We call this 'half past'.

If the short hand is between 4 and 5, the time is 4:30 or half past 4.
cards.hint: 6 is halfway around the clock, so it means half past

cards.front: How do you read time like 7:45?
cards.back:
7:45 means 7 hours and 45 minutes.

The short hand will be between 7 and 8 (close to 8).
The long hand will be on 9 (because 9 x 5 = 45 minutes).

We can also say 'quarter to 8' because 45 minutes is 15 minutes before the next hour.
cards.hint: 45 minutes = 15 minutes before the next hour

cards.front: How many minutes are in 1 hour?
cards.back:
There are 60 minutes in 1 hour.

The minute hand goes all the way around the clock once in 60 minutes.

So 1 hour = 60 minutes
Half hour = 30 minutes
Quarter hour = 15 minutes
cards.hint: Count by 5s all the way around: 5, 10, 15... up to 60

decks.name: AM/PM and Elapsed Time
decks.description: Learn about AM and PM, and how to calculate time that has passed

cards.front: What do AM and PM mean?
cards.back:
AM means the time from midnight (12:00 at night) to just before noon (12:00 in the afternoon).

PM means the time from noon (12:00 in the afternoon) to just before midnight.

School starts at 8:00 AM (morning).
You eat dinner at about 8:00 PM (evening).
cards.hint: AM = morning time, PM = afternoon and night time

cards.front: How many hours are in one full day?
cards.back:
There are 24 hours in one full day.

12 hours of AM (midnight to noon)
12 hours of PM (noon to midnight)

The hour hand goes around the clock twice in one day!
cards.hint: The clock shows 12 hours, but a day has twice that many

cards.back:
Elapsed time is the amount of time that passes between a start time and an end time.

If a cricket match starts at 2:00 PM and ends at 5:00 PM, the elapsed time is 3 hours.
cards.hint: Think: how much time has gone by from start to finish?

cards.front: School starts at 8:00 AM and ends at 2:00 PM. How long is the school day?
cards.back:
Count from 8:00 AM to 2:00 PM:
8 to 9 = 1 hour
9 to 10 = 2 hours
10 to 11 = 3 hours
11 to 12 = 4 hours
12 to 1 = 5 hours
1 to 2 = 6 hours

The school day is 6 hours long!
cards.hint: Count the hours from start to end on your fingers

cards.front: If a movie starts at 4:30 PM and is 2 hours long, what time does it end?
cards.back:
Start at 4:30 PM and add 2 hours:
4:30 + 1 hour = 5:30
5:30 + 1 hour = 6:30

The movie ends at 6:30 PM.
cards.hint: Add the hours one by one to the start time

decks.name: Calendar Reading
decks.description: Learn to read calendars and understand days, months, and years

cards.front: How many days are in a week? Name them.
cards.back:
There are 7 days in a week:

Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday.

In India, the week often starts with Monday on calendars.

cards.front: How many months are in a year? Which months have 30 days and which have 31?
cards.back:
There are 12 months in a year.

31 days: January, March, May, July, August, October, December (7 months)
30 days: April, June, September, November (4 months)
28 or 29 days: February (28 normally, 29 in a leap year)
cards.hint: Use the knuckle trick: knuckle months have 31 days, dips have 30

cards.front: How many days are in a year?
cards.back:
A normal year has 365 days.
A leap year has 366 days (February gets an extra day, making it 29 days).

A leap year comes once every 4 years. The years 2024 and 2028 are leap years.
cards.hint: Most years have 365 days, but every 4 years there is one extra day

cards.front: If today is Wednesday, what day will it be after 10 days?
cards.back:
Count forward 10 days from Wednesday.
Since a week has 7 days, 10 days = 1 week + 3 days.
One week later is also Wednesday. Then 3 more days = Thursday, Friday, Saturday.

So after 10 days it will be Saturday!
cards.hint: Divide by 7 to find the extra days after complete weeks

cards.front: How do you read a date on a calendar?
cards.back:
A calendar shows the month and year at the top. Days of the week are in columns. The numbers are the dates.

To find what day March 15 falls on, find 15 in the March calendar and look at the column heading. That tells you the day of the week!
cards.hint: Find the date number and check which day column it falls under
